info.title: OpenAPI Plant Store
info.description: A sample API that uses a plant store as an example to demonstrate features in the OpenAPI specification. Workspace usage endpoints document the Tess agent execution history API.

tags.name: Usage
tags.description: Workspace agent execution history and usage reporting.

get.description:
Returns a paginated list of agent executions for a workspace (current `user_openai` rows, archived executions, and edited chats), with optional filters for date range, agent type, and user. Results are cached for 60 seconds per query key. Pagination uses a `has_more` pattern (one extra row is fetched to detect the next page).

**Auth stack (pre-production):** Sanctum (`auth:sanctum`), Tess workspace API access (`checkApiWorkspaceAccess`), and token ability `use_agents`.

**Workspace header:** Send numeric workspace id in `x-workspace-id`. Missing or invalid values yield **422** with a JSON `message`.

**Filtering by another user's `user_id`:** Only allowed if the authenticated user has the workspace permission configured in `WORKSPACE_DOCUMENTS_READ`. Otherwise the scope is forced to the current user only; supplying another user's id returns **403**.

**Date rules:** Either use `range` (`1d`, `7d`, `30d`) when `start_date`/`end_date` are omitted (default effective range is **30d**), or send both `start_date` and `end_date` (`YYYY-MM-DD`) with `end_date` ≥ `start_date` and span ≤ **90** days. With `range`, the window is relative to the end of the current day. A feature flag may clamp the effective start to a configured minimum date (silent adjustment).

**Data sources:** UNION of current executions, `user_openai_archives`, and `user_openai_edits`, ordered by `created_at` desc, then `id` desc.

parameters.name: x-workspace-id
parameters.description: Numeric workspace id. Required for business logic; invalid or missing values produce **422**.

parameters.name: range
parameters.description: Relative window: `1d`, `7d`, or `30d`. Used only when **both** `start_date` and `end_date` are omitted. If no date parameters are sent, behavior defaults to **30d**.

parameters.name: start_date
parameters.description: Start of inclusive date range (`YYYY-MM-DD`). Must be sent together with `end_date` (`required_with`).

parameters.name: end_date
parameters.description: End of inclusive date range (`YYYY-MM-DD`). Must be ≥ `start_date`. Range span must not exceed 90 days.

parameters.name: user_id
parameters.description: Filter executions by user. Filtering by another user requires `WORKSPACE_DOCUMENTS_READ` for the workspace; otherwise **403**.

parameters.name: type
parameters.description: Agent type filter. `all` or omitted = no type filter.

items.name: Support agent
items.output: This content is only available on Tess

403.description: JSON `{"message": "..."}` — user not associated with the workspace, or `user_id` targets another user without `WORKSPACE_DOCUMENTS_READ`. Message may be empty depending on the exception.

422.description: Laravel validation errors for query parameters; or JSON `{"message": "..."}` for invalid/missing workspace, invalid dates, or date range exceeding 90 days.

get.description: Returns all plants from the system that the user has access to

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: The maximum number of results to return

post.description: Creates a new plant in the store

requestBody.description: Plant to add to the store

delete.description: Deletes a single plant based on the ID supplied

post.description: Information about a new plant added to the store

200.description: Return a 200 status to indicate that the data was received successfully

has_more.description: True if another page exists. The API requests `per_page + 1` rows; if the extra row exists, `has_more` is true and only the first `per_page` rows are returned in `items`.

UsageTokens.description: Present only when `detailed_credits` decodes to `metric === "token"`. Otherwise `tokens` is null.

WorkspaceUsageItem.description: Single execution row after enrichment. Base fields align with `ExportExecutionsByWorkspaceJob::getExecutionsData`; `used_model`, `tokens`, and `link` are added by `WorkspaceUsageService`.

oneOf.description: Synthetic id for edited chats, e.g. `chat-edited-{user_openai_id}`.

id.description: Execution id, archive id from JSON, or synthetic `chat-edited-*` for edits.

type.description: OpenAI agent type from `openai.type` (e.g. chat, image, text, voiceover, video, code).

status.description: `succeeded` when `user_openai.status` indicates success; otherwise `failed` (including empty status).

email.description: Email of the user who ran the execution.

credits.description: Credits for the execution; **0** if status is not `succeeded`.

output.description: **current:** for `image`, `video`, `voiceover`, the real output; for other types, fixed text `This content is only available on Tess`. **archived:** `This item was deleted.` **edited:** `This item was edited.`

used_model.description: **current:** `user_openai.used_model` when column exists. **archived:** `data.used_model` in archive JSON. **edited:** always null in current logic.

tokens.description: Token breakdown when billing metric is token-based; often null for image/video/voiceover.

link.description: **archived** / **edited:** always null. **current chat:** app path `/dashboard/user/ai/chat/ai-chat/{slug}?_chat_id={id}` using `root_id` when set, else row `id`; omitted for invalid ids or ids starting with `chat-edited-`. **current image/video/voiceover:** signed/public URL from storage when `output` is non-empty and does not contain “content is only available” (case insensitive). Other types: null.
